methodsAttempted.query: site:unimas.my UNIMAS generative AI policy OR guidelines OR artificial intelligence students
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl returned official Gazette, repository, CDO, and national-guideline hits. Research and event pages were not used as claim evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unimas.my "ChatGPT" "UNIMAS" policy OR guideline OR academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Search results were primarily UNIMAS Institutional Repository records about ChatGPT research rather than operational university guidance.
Search identified the official UNIMAS CDO AI Hub and a hosted national AI governance PDF.
Search surfaced the official Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities 2025/2026 student handbook PDF containing an AI-use section.
robots.txt allows the handbook PDF path; direct HTTP fetch returned 200 OK with application/pdf.

artifacts.summary: Official discovery found one substantive faculty-scoped AI guidance source: the Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities 2025/2026 student handbook. CDO AI Hub, Gazette, repository, national guideline, and library pages were rejected for this bundle because they were initiative, event, research, external, or low-policy-specificity sources.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Buku Panduan Pelajar / Student Handbook 2025/2026 - Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official FSSH handbook PDF contains section 13, Tatacara Penggunaan Kecerdasan Buatan (AI) Sebagai Pelajar Universiti, with specific AI-use purposes, good practices, limitations, and academic-integrity cautions.
Official CDO page describes AI Hub structure, outcomes, launch materials, and links to external guidelines, but it does not state binding student or staff AI-use rules.

artifacts.sourceTitle: The National Guidelines on AI Governance & Ethics

Official library page links external publisher resources about generative AI and publishing ethics but does not itself state UNIMAS AI-use policy.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The page describes AI Hub structure, outcomes, launch media, and external guidelines such as national and UNESCO guidance.
artifacts.notes: Useful context for UNIMAS AI activity, but not enough to support binding policy or student academic-integrity claims.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The story says a workshop titled Generative AI for Teachers & Students was held and describes expert commentary on educational uses of generative AI.
artifacts.notes: Official news article, but not a policy or guidance document.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The repository abstract says the chapter explores use of ChatGPT for academic support and points to a need for universities to provide explicit AI guidelines.
artifacts.notes: Research finding is not a UNIMAS rule and should not be converted into a policy claim.

artifacts.notes: Hosted by UNIMAS CDO as an external guideline, but not authored as a UNIMAS-specific policy.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The page links external Wiley and Nature resources about generative AI, open access, and publishing ethics.
artifacts.notes: Official library resource page, but the AI material is external reading context and not a local UNIMAS policy.

targets.sourceTitle: Buku Panduan Pelajar / Student Handbook 2025/2026 - Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities

stopConditions: Stop after collecting the official FSSH handbook AI section with extractable body text.
Do not infer university-wide policy from this faculty handbook.
Reject research papers, event news, external national guidelines, and generic AI initiative pages as claim evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Buku Panduan Pelajar / Student Handbook 2025/2026 - Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities

artifacts.claimText: The UNIMAS Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities 2025/2026 student handbook says students may use AI to support learning through note summaries, language checking, additional references, subject understanding, interactive exercises, data analysis, project planning, and report writing.

citation.citationTitle: Buku Panduan Pelajar / Student Handbook 2025/2026 - Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities
citation.publisher: Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The UNIMAS Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities 2025/2026 student handbook frames good AI practice as using AI as a support tool, checking AI responses against academic sources, using AI to sharpen critical thinking, and recognizing that AI responses may be inaccurate or unsuitable.

citation.citationTitle: Buku Panduan Pelajar / Student Handbook 2025/2026 - Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities
citation.publisher: Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities, Universiti Malaysia Sarawak
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The UNIMAS Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities 2025/2026 student handbook says AI does not replace lecturers, lecture notes, or main academic references, and warns students not to copy directly from AI answers, submit AI answers as their own work without citation, or use AI to cheat in exams or assignments.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Buku Panduan Pelajar / Student Handbook 2025/2026 - Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 13.1 Tujuan Penggunaan AI: a. Membantu pembelajaran dengan membuat ringkasan nota, menyemak bahasa dan memberi rujukan tambahan. b. Meningkatkan pemahaman subjek melalui soalan, latihan dan aktiviti interaktif. c. Menyokong tugasan seperti analisis data, perancangan projek, dan penulisan laporan.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Section 13.1 says AI may support learning through summaries, language checks, additional references, understanding, interactive exercises, data analysis, project planning, and report writing.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Buku Panduan Pelajar / Student Handbook 2025/2026 - Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 13.2 Amalan Baik: a. Menggunakan AI sebagai alat sokongan, bukan untuk menggantikan usaha sendiri. b. Menyemak jawapan atau maklumat daripada AI dengan sumber rujukan akademik. c. Menggunakan AI untuk mengasah pemikiran kritis, bukan menyalin jawapan bulat-bulat. d. Mengetahui bahawa jawapan AI mungkin tidak sentiasa tepat atau sesuai.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Section 13.2 frames good practice as support use, academic-source checking, critical thinking, avoiding direct copying, and recognizing that AI answers may be inaccurate or unsuitable.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Buku Panduan Pelajar / Student Handbook 2025/2026 - Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 13.3 Limitasi: a. AI tidak boleh menggantikan pensyarah, nota kuliah, atau rujukan akademik utama. b. Elakkan plagiat dengan tidak menyalin secara terus daripada jawapan AI. 13.4 Integriti Akademik: a. Plagiat ialah kesalahan akademik yang serius. Jangan serahkan jawapan AI sebagai hasil kerja sendiri tanpa menyatakan rujukan. b. Tidak menggunakan AI untuk menipu dalam peperiksaan atau tugasan.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Sections 13.3 and 13.4 say AI does not replace lecturers, notes, or main academic references; students should avoid plagiarism, not submit AI answers as their own without citation, and not use AI to cheat in exams or assignments.

artifacts.title: Universiti Malaysia Sarawak (UNIMAS) AI Guidance Candidate
artifacts.summary: Staged three agent-reviewed, faculty-scoped AI-use claims for the UNIMAS Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities 2025/2026 student handbook. The evidence supports FSSH student handbook guidance only; it does not establish a UNIMAS-wide AI policy.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims are scoped to the Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities student handbook and should not be interpreted as a university-wide policy without additional official evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
